1.name: Bike Sweet Spot — 2×15 min
1.description: Sweet spot training at 88–94% FTP (Zone 3–4). Warm up 15 min, 2×15 min at sweet spot with 5 min recovery. Cool down 10 min.

1.name: Race-Specific Aerobic Swim — 900 yd
1.description: Warm up 300 yd. Race rhythm session: groove stroke quality early, then hold sustainable pacing. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 5×100 yd at sustainable race-swim rhythm (15 sec rest). Where the lane allows, merge into longer unbroken 200–400 yd pieces. Sight every 6–8 strokes; practice breathing to both sides for chop or sun glare. Swim these repeats hands-only by default; paddles optional for a few repeats only if shoulders feel good—not the entire main set. Cool down 200 yd.

1.name: Easy Ride — 1.0 hr
1.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

1.name: Sweet-Spot Run — 3 mi at moderate effort
1.description: Warm up 1.5 mi easy, then 3 mi at sustained moderate effort (~RPE 6, conversational in short sentences — meaningfully harder than your easy runs but not threshold). Cool down 1.5 mi easy. Builds aerobic durability without the recovery cost of intervals.

1.name: Swim Threshold — 1100 yd
1.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 4×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

1.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
1.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

1.name: Long Ride — 2.0 hr
1.description: Aerobic endurance ride at Z2. Maintain 60–70 rpm cadence. Nutrition practice: eat every 40–45 minutes. No surges.

1.name: Long Run — 8.5 mi
1.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

2.name: Easy Swim — 1050 yd
2.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Efficiency-first: sharpen one or two mechanics before aerobic volume so every yard transfers to the race. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Fingertip Drag (high-elbow recovery). 4×150 yd at easy aerobic pace. Focus on technique: high elbow catch, bilateral breathing. Cool down 200 yd.

9.name: Bike Threshold — 2×20 min
9.description: Warm up 15 min with fast-pedal spins. 2×20 min at FTP (Zone 4 — hard but sustainable). 5 min easy between. Cool down 10 min.

9.name: Mixed/Fartlek Swim — 1300 yd
9.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 3×400 yd building intensity within each 400 — first 100 easy aerobic, second 100 moderate, third 100 race-rhythm, fourth 100 hard but controlled. 30 sec rest between. Cool down 200 yd easy.

9.name: Easy Ride — 0.8 hr
9.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

9.name: Tempo Run — 3 mi at threshold
9.description: Warm up 1.5 mi easy, then 3 mi at lactate threshold (comfortably hard — 7–8 RPE, can say a few words). Cool down 1.5 mi easy.

9.name: Swim Threshold — 1600 yd
9.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing). 6×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

9.name: Easy Run — 3 mi
9.description: Recovery run. Fully conversational Z1–Z2 pace. These miles build your aerobic base without adding meaningful fatigue.

9.name: Brick — Bike 2.0 hr
9.description: Brick bike at Zone 2. Build leg feel for the transition. Maintain steady power throughout.

9.name: Brick — Run 3.5 mi off the bike
9.description: Immediately after the bike. The first 5 min will feel strange — focus on turnover, not pace. Easy Z2 throughout.

9.name: Long Run — 10 mi
9.description: Aerobic long run at conversational pace. Full Z2 effort — if you can't speak in sentences, slow down.

11.name: Bike Threshold — 3×20 min
11.description: Warm up 15 min with fast-pedal spins. 3×20 min at FTP (Zone 4 — hard but sustainable). 5 min easy between. Cool down 10 min.

11.name: Swim Time Trial — 1400 yd
11.description: Warm up 500 yd with progressive build (last 100 at moderate effort). 400 yd MAX effort — sustained hard, hold form. 4 min easy recovery (back-and-forth jogs or float). 200 yd MAX effort — leave nothing. Cool down 300 yd easy. The engine will recompute your 100yd pace target from these two splits.

11.name: Easy Ride — 1.0 hr
11.description: Recovery spin at Z1–Z2. No pushing. Legs should feel loose and refreshed by the end.

Prescribed drills: 2×50 Catch-Up (front-quadrant timing); 4×50 Fist Swim (early vertical forearm). 5×150 yd at easy aerobic pace. Focus on technique: high elbow catch, bilateral breathing. Cool down 200 yd.

13.name: Bike VO2max — 3×5 min

13.name: Race-Pace Sustained Swim — 800 yd
13.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. 3×600 yd at race pace (sustainable hard — what you can hold for the race-distance swim). 45 sec rest between. Cool down 300 yd easy.

13.name: Easy Ride — 1.4 hr

13.name: Half-Marathon Pace Run — 3 mi
13.description: 1.5 mi warm-up, 3 mi at 70.3 / half-marathon run pace (Z3–Z4), 1.5 mi cool-down. Trains your body to hold race effort on accumulating fatigue.

13.name: Swim Threshold — 1300 yd
13.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. Hard swim day: prime stroke integrity before high-output repeats. Prescribed drills: 2×50 Single-Arm Freestyle (rotation + balanced pull). 4×100 yd at hard effort (maximal sustainable — what you can hold for the interval, not past it) with 15 sec rest. 3×150 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

17.name: Bike Openers — 30 min
17.description: Short pre-race sharpener. 20 min easy Z2, then 3×30-second fast-pedal bursts. Legs should feel snappy afterwards.

17.name: Race-Specific Aerobic Swim — 800 yd
17.description: Warm up 300 yd. 5×100 yd at sustainable race-swim rhythm (15 sec rest). Where the lane allows, merge into longer unbroken 200–400 yd pieces. 17.name: Race-Week Activation Swim — 800 yd
17.description: Warm up 300 yd easy. 4×50 yd build accelerations (start easy, finish strong; long rest between — pure neuromuscular sharpener, NOT a hard interval). 2×100 yd easy aerobic. Cool down 200 yd.

17.name: Test 70.3 race
17.description: Race day. Swim 1.2mi → Bike 56mi → Run 13.1mi. No add-on training; execute pacing and fueling.

9: Week 9 long ride is shorter than the typical build floor (2h vs 2.25h). Lengthening toward 2.25h+ builds the bike-leg endurance the race demands.
11: Week 11 long ride is shorter than the typical build floor (2.25h vs 2.5h). Lengthening toward 2.5h+ builds the bike-leg endurance the race demands.
13: Week 13 has no long run scheduled — race-specific plans target a 11+ mi long run for durability.
Week 13 long ride is shorter than the typical race-specific floor (2.25h vs 2.5h). Lengthening toward 2.5h+ builds the bike-leg endurance the race demands.
15: Week 15 long ride is shorter than the typical race-specific floor (2.25h vs 2.75h). Lengthening toward 2.75h+ builds the bike-leg endurance the race demands.
